judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent correctly filtered 2015 metadata and joined to article texts, but in execute_python (e.g., call function-call-1723862692206953347) it used a naive keyword-based classifier with a fallback to "World" to determine categories, misclassifying articles and producing a wrong top region (North America) instead of the ground-truth Africa.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly retrieving 2015 article_ids with regions and all article titles/descriptions, the agent used a naive substring-based keyword classifier in execute_python (calls function-call-13901013144232453965 and function-call-12718512423718023595) to label "World" articles—e.g., matching short substrings without word boundaries—misclassifying categories and yielding incorrect region counts that led it to return "South America" instead of the ground-truth "Africa."
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly pulling 2015 article metadata and all articles, the agent’s final execute_python step (function-call-15290285311598642316) used an ad-hoc keyword/tie-breaker classifier that deprioritized "World" and expanded Business/World keywords, misclassifying articles and yielding South America as the max region instead of the correct Africa.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly retrieving 2015 article metadata and article texts, the agent used an ad hoc keyword classifier in execute_python (call id function-call-15428933876846048942) that defaults unclassified items to "World" and employs broad "World" keywords, thereby misclassifying many articles and skewing the regional counts to "North America" instead of the correct "Africa."
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent computed "World" counts in two batches—first using a limited Mongo query over 13–127570 (function-call-3329559544456987844) and then adding counts from IDs >100000 (function-call-1071897199243019569) in execute_python (function-call-7298384671893793561) without deduplicating overlap with the first batch, which double-counted some articles and incorrectly shifted the lead from Africa to South America.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
